paragraphs.body: In the article, Dogs look like their owners – it’s a scientific fact, David Robson shares some important facts based on experimentation on why dogs tend to look like their owners to put our wonders to rest. Based on Joshua Roy's experiment in a public dog park, we can confirm that pets look like their owners. He asked strangers to try and match dogs to their owners. They succeeded with most of them because the ones they couldn’t match up were mixed breeds. First, Robson clarifies that we mostly see similarities in pure-bred dogs, but this has its reason too. It is stated that the reason is due to the attraction of familiarity. When people go to the adoption center or the person selling the dogs, they are more likely to choose one with the same personality or even physical characteristics. Next, Robson mentions that personality has a big role in explaining why the similarity between humans and dogs exists because both the owner and the dog learn from each other since they live in the same environment. The time we spend with dogs creates a relationship that is different than with friends or partners. This could be confirmed because it is known for a fact that most dogs last longer than relationships with partners. Finally, David Robson reminds us that dogs are a man’s best friend, and they are a better reflection of our true nature.

paragraphs.body: The term reflection is very important throughout this article because every fact about why dogs tend to look like humans has to do with some type of reflection whether physical or behavioral. A way Robson explains how we end up with a reflection of ourselves is due to familiarity. David Robson states, “Maybe this is all due to the allure of familiarity: a dog may seem more comforting if it resembles the other members of our family, who we know and love.” In simpler words, we are going to find a family member, not just a dog we are going to take care of. Robson also describes that the same way we find mates that look like us we will choose a dog that resembles us. Therefore, we would prefer anything that looks just a little bit like us. Going back to reading about adopting or buying a dog that looks like another person, reminded me of my sister’s experience while she was in college.

annotations.text: has to do with some type of reflection
annotations.comment: interesting. I wonder if this word might have some deeper meaning and value for your essay? Let's see :)

paragraphs.body: My sister went to college in Indiana in a quiet area, empty, and not a lot of school spirit. Don’t get me wrong, she had a good experience, but it was a big change. Imagine coming from a place where everything is about being together with family, friends, and your pets to a lonely dorm, quiet campus, and no pets. One day my mom and I received a call from my sister, and there she was with Slushee, a joyful border collie mixed with Australian shepherd. It was a surprise for everyone because my sister mentioned she wanted to adopt a dog. After all, she was feeling lonely, but we never thought she would adopt while studying. What I didn’t know was that my sister was going through anxiety and depression. She always told me how much fun she was having because, obviously as the big sister she is, she was not going to tell me she was battling with anxiety and depression. Slushee was signed up as a service dog and she helped my sister get her bachelor’s degree. Everything was okay until my sister moved to her first apartment. Slushee started having symptoms that worried my sister. When she took Slushee to the vet, my sister was shocked when the veterinarian informed her Slushee had anxiety. They had to prescribe pills to help her with the anxiety. Now my sister jokes around saying that they are made for each other because they both were taking medication for the same thing and at the same time.

annotations.text: They had to prescribe pills to help her with the anxiety. Now my sister jokes around saying that they are made for each other

paragraphs.body: While reading Robson’s article, my mind went straight to her experience. The author states a quote that the bond is strong we don’t realize how much it affects the relationship between the owner and the dog. David reminds us, “Today, they look like us, act like us, and – unlike other humans – they always reciprocate our feelings.” In other words, they end up feeling how we are feeling. Slushee took my sister’s anxiety away, but she ended up with it. I always wondered how that happened, but Robson’s article made it clear that since your pet lives in the same environment as you, they learn about your personality the same way you learn about theirs.

paragraphs.body: My family and I had another dog that impacted our lives greatly. I grew up with this dog like we were siblings. I was one year older than he was. Each family member had a special relationship with Polo, but my mom’s relationship with him was the strongest. My mom picked him out of the basket. Polo was the only dark golden out of all his brothers and sisters. That grabbed my mom’s attention and brought him home. It was a surprise for my sister and me, but I don’t remember because I was a toddler. My mom has a lot of stories about Polo when he was a puppy. She even told us that he fell from the second floor and survived. When he was an adult dog, the only person he respected was my mom. He followed instructions from everyone, but he greatly respected my mom. Every time we had visitors, Polo stayed beside my mom like “protecting her” from the visitors. I never understood why they always had a stronger relationship than with any other family member. By the time Polo was 10 years old, I could find some similarities between him and my mom. For example, they had the same hair color. My mom always used to say how she tried to paint her hair a certain way, and it would turn the same color as before again. I can’t believe how it is possible how a dog and a human to learn so much about each other and have a bond that ends up having physical similarities.

annotations.text: She even told us that he fell from the second floor and survived.

paragraphs.body: I know that I will never forget Marco Polo and Slushee. These two dogs have impacted my life in a way I would have never imagined a dog could. They showed me the true definition of why dogs are a man’s best friend. Slushee went through such a difficult time, but she was still happy seeing my sister happy. Every day when my sister arrived from work, she waited for her at the door and when she saw her, she jumped on her and did her cute, little wiggles. Also, I have always said that the first dog I will have when I am older will be a golden retriever. I know that the dog I will have in the future won’t be Polo, but at least it can help me miss him less.
Works Cited David Robson. “Dogs look like their owners – it’s a scientific fact” BBC https://www.bbc.com/future/article/20151111-why-do-dogs-look-like-their-owners
